reason: The rule ensures that the pam_faillock module uses a persistent directory for storing failure timestamps, so that account lockout information survives reboots. Setting the 'dir' option in /etc/security/faillock.conf to a durable location preserves audit data and prevents accidental loss of lockout tracking after a system restart.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; Misconfiguration could cause pam_faillock to fail, disabling lockout tracking; Incorrect directory path may lead to missing or inaccessible failure records
